label: The Storyteller
gift: Poignance — weaving loss into meaning and grief into belonging.

cards.name: Catch the Story You're Telling
cards.essence: Notice the story you're already telling yourself before it runs the show.
steps: Catch the narration running under the moment.
Feel where it grips in your body.
Name it as a story, not a fact — 'I'm telling myself that…'.
cards.primaryQuestion: What story am I already inside, and is it true?
cards.campaignQuestion: What story is quietly shaping how I see this?
cards.optimizesFor: Catching the narrative before it drives the move.
forbiddenMoves: Mistaking the story for the facts
Performing a tidier story than the real one
Pressuring the feeling into a neat arc
cards.remediation: Say the story out loud, then ask what part you actually know.

tell.working: You can feel the story loosen its grip.
tell.performed: You polish the story instead of questioning it.

contraindications: When you need to act now, not narrate
cards.example: You notice you're telling yourself 'no one will care' — you name it as a story, not truth.

cards.name: Sense the Meaning Surfacing
cards.essence: Sense the meaning trying to surface in the room before you shape it.
steps: Listen for what the moment is reaching toward.
Feel the charge in the room in your body.
Name the meaning lightly, as a question, not a verdict.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I imposing my meaning, or sensing theirs?
cards.campaignQuestion: What meaning is the group reaching for that no one has named?
cards.optimizesFor: Surfacing shared meaning instead of imposing a script.
forbiddenMoves: Hijacking the room's story
Performing insight
Pressuring people into your frame
cards.remediation: Offer the meaning as 'I wonder if this is about…' and let them correct you.
contraindications: When the room needs action, not interpretation
cards.example: In a tense meeting you name 'I wonder if this is really about feeling unseen' — and the room exhales.

cards.name: Notice the story you are already telling — Edge
cards.essence: In yourself: poignance — loss into meaning, at the edge, naming what resists.
steps: Notice what is actually here — at the edge, naming what resists.
Turn it inward: the story you are already telling.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I not letting myself notice here?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this situation need someone to see?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing what is actually there before reacting — your poignance — loss into meaning at the Edge level.
forbiddenMoves: Reacting before you have actually looked
Sliding into the Performer — spectacle over truth.
failureModes: Blind spots you swear aren't there
cards.remediation: Name one thing you hadn't let yourself see.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Performer — spectacle over truth.

steps: Notice what is actually here — at the edge, naming what resists.
Bring it to others: the meaning trying to surface.

cards.name: Feel the grief beneath the story — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: poignance — loss into meaning, sensing what is here.
steps: Receive it without flinching — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: the grief beneath the story.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I actually feeling, underneath?
cards.campaignQuestion: What is the other person or group really reaching for?
cards.optimizesFor: Receiving the charge instead of bracing against it — your poignance — loss into meaning at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Numbing or performing the feeling instead of feeling it
Sliding into the Performer — spectacle over truth.
cards.remediation: Let it land for one breath before you respond.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Performer — spectacle over truth.

cards.essence: For others: poignance — loss into meaning, sensing what is here.
steps: Receive it without flinching — sensing what is here.

cards.name: Feel the Grief Under It
cards.essence: Let yourself feel the loss beneath the story before you tell anyone.
steps: Drop under the storyline to the feeling.
Let the grief or tenderness be in your body without fixing it.
Stay until it has something to say.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I grieving that the story is covering?
cards.campaignQuestion: What loss underneath this haven't I let myself feel?
cards.optimizesFor: Telling from real feeling, not performed emotion.
forbiddenMoves: Skipping to the moral
Performing grief you don't feel
Turning your pain into others' obligation
failureModes: A polished story with no pulse
cards.remediation: Put words to the feeling for yourself first, privately.

tell.performed: You rehearse how the grief will look.

contraindications: When you're not safe enough to open yet
cards.example: Before sharing why this matters, you sit with the actual loss for three breaths.

cards.name: Hold What It Means to Them
cards.essence: Receive what this means to the people in it before shaping a story.
steps: Ask what it means to them and actually listen.
Feel their meaning land in your body.
Reflect it back in their words, not yours.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I making space for their meaning or mine?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this mean to the people most affected?
cards.optimizesFor: A story rooted in their truth and consent, not your spin.
forbiddenMoves: Speaking for people
Using their pain as content
Implying they owe you their story
cards.remediation: Ask 'did I get that right?' and let them rewrite it.
contraindications: When they haven't consented to share
cards.example: You ask someone what the moment meant to them, then echo their exact words back.

cards.name: Find your own true meaning first — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: poignance — loss into meaning, sensing what is here.
steps: Transform the charge — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: your own true meaning first.
cards.primaryQuestion: What charge am I carrying that needs to move?
cards.campaignQuestion: What needs to be metabolized before we move together?
cards.optimizesFor: Metabolizing the charge so it stops leaking — your poignance — loss into meaning at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Venting the charge onto someone else
Sliding into the Performer — spectacle over truth.
cards.remediation: Name what you are feeling out loud before you act.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Performer — spectacle over truth.

cards.name: Find Your True Meaning First
cards.essence: Distill what this actually means to you before you offer meaning to anyone.
steps: Sift the event for the one true thing it taught you.
Feel whether it rings true in the body.
Write it as one honest sentence.
cards.primaryQuestion: What's the real meaning here, under the meaning I'd perform?
cards.campaignQuestion: What durable truth am I taking from this?
cards.optimizesFor: Meaning that's earned, not manufactured.
forbiddenMoves: Forcing a tidy lesson
Performing wisdom
Pressuring an arc onto raw experience
failureModes: A moral that isn't true yet
cards.remediation: If the sentence feels like a slogan, sit longer.

tell.performed: You reach for the quotable version.

contraindications: When it's too soon to know the meaning
cards.example: Instead of 'everything happens for a reason,' you write the smaller true thing you learned.

cards.name: Weave Loss into Belonging
cards.essence: Find the thread that turns a shared loss into shared meaning and belonging.
steps: Name what was lost, honestly.
Find what it reveals that still matters to everyone.
Offer the thread that lets people hold both, and feel whether it lands.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I bypassing the grief to get to the uplift?
cards.campaignQuestion: What meaning lets this community carry the loss together?
cards.optimizesFor: Collective meaning that honors the loss instead of erasing it.
forbiddenMoves: Premature silver-linings
Spinning loss into a brand
Guilt-tripping people into hope
cards.remediation: Let the grief be witnessed before offering the meaning.

tell.working: People feel both held and moved.
tell.performed: You skip to the inspirational close.

contraindications: When the wound is too fresh for meaning-making
cards.example: At a hard moment you name the loss fully, then the bond it revealed — not a tidy lesson.

cards.name: Deepen your honesty over performance — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: poignance — loss into meaning, sensing what is here.
steps: Build the capacity — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: your honesty over performance.
cards.primaryQuestion: Who do I need to become for this?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity does this work need us to build?
cards.optimizesFor: Building the capacity the work keeps asking for — your poignance — loss into meaning at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Forcing growth or skipping the rep
Sliding into the Performer — spectacle over truth.
cards.remediation: Pick one small rep and do it once.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Performer — spectacle over truth.

cards.name: Deepen the shared story’s roots — Sense
cards.essence: For others: poignance — loss into meaning, sensing what is here.
steps: Build the capacity — sensing what is here.

cards.remediation: Pick one small rep and do it once.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Performer — spectacle over truth.

steps: Build the capacity — at the edge, naming what resists.
Bring it to others: the shared story’s roots.
cards.primaryQuestion: Who do I need to become for this?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity does this work need us to build?
cards.optimizesFor: Building the capacity the work keeps asking for — your poignance — loss into meaning at the Edge level.
forbiddenMoves: Forcing growth or skipping the rep
Sliding into the Performer — spectacle over truth.
cards.remediation: Pick one small rep and do it once.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Performer — spectacle over truth.

cards.name: Deepen Your Honest Voice
cards.essence: Practice telling it true over time, so honesty outgrows performance.
steps: Notice where you reach for effect instead of truth.
Tell one story this week stripped of polish.
Feel the difference in your body afterward.
cards.primaryQuestion: Where am I performing instead of telling it true?
cards.campaignQuestion: What practice grows my honest voice?
cards.optimizesFor: A voice people trust because it's true, not slick.
forbiddenMoves: Practicing the performance
Chasing applause
Pressuring yourself to be profound
cards.remediation: Cut the most impressive line; keep the truest one.

tell.working: It costs a little and feels more real.

contraindications: When rawness would harm, not serve
cards.example: You tell a story without the punchline you usually lean on, and it lands deeper.

cards.name: Steward the Shared Story
cards.essence: Tend the group's story over time so it stays true as things change.
steps: Notice where the shared story has gone stale or false.
Update it with the people, not for them — feel where it still rings true.
Keep the thread that still holds; let the rest evolve.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I clinging to a story that no longer serves us?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we keep our story honest as we grow?
cards.optimizesFor: A living narrative the group still recognizes as theirs.
forbiddenMoves: Freezing the founding myth
Rewriting history alone
Guilt-tripping people with the old story
cards.remediation: Invite the group to retell it in their own words.

tell.working: People feel ownership of the story.
tell.performed: You defend your version of the legend.

contraindications: When the old story is load-bearing and shouldn't change yet
cards.example: You gather the crew to retell why you started — and let the story update.

cards.name: Name what this means to you — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: poignance — loss into meaning, sensing what is here.
steps: Take the concrete act — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: what this means to you.
cards.primaryQuestion: What is the one act I keep avoiding?
cards.campaignQuestion: What concrete act would actually help right now?
cards.optimizesFor: Taking the one concrete act that moves things — your poignance — loss into meaning at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Hinting or prepping forever instead of acting
Sliding into the Performer — spectacle over truth.
failureModes: The move that never quite happens
cards.remediation: Do the smallest real version today.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Performer — spectacle over truth.

cards.name: Tell It Without Centering Yourself
cards.essence: Tell the story in a way that serves the room, not your image.
steps: Check your motive: am I sharing to serve or to be seen?
Feel the nerves without letting them drive performance.
Tell it plainly, then get out of the way.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I telling this for them or for me?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do I tell this so the focus stays where it belongs?
cards.optimizesFor: A telling that points at the cause, not the teller.
forbiddenMoves: Making yourself the hero
Fishing for sympathy
Pressuring the room to react
cards.remediation: Cut every line that's about how you look.

tell.working: Afterward the room talks about the cause, not you.
tell.performed: You scan for who was impressed.

contraindications: When your own story IS the point and consent is clear
cards.example: You share your part briefly, then turn the room toward the people it's about.

cards.name: Name What This Means, for the Room
cards.essence: Give the room a story it can hold and act from — true, shared, and inviting.
steps: Name the meaning in one clear line everyone can feel and carry.
Make room for others to add their thread.
End with an opening to act, not a mic-drop.
cards.primaryQuestion: Is this a story that includes them, or a performance at them?
cards.campaignQuestion: What story gives this group something true to rally around?
cards.optimizesFor: A shared story that invites participation, with consent.
forbiddenMoves: Monologuing
Using anyone's story without consent
Guilt-tripping the room into action
failureModes: The speech that impresses but doesn't move
cards.remediation: Ask 'what would you add?' and mean it.

tell.working: People pick up the story and run with it.
tell.performed: You land the applause line and stop.

contraindications: When the room needs quiet, not a story
cards.example: You name the one thing it's really about — then invite others to add why it matters to them.

cards.name: Tell the Story You'll Stand Behind
cards.essence: Tell it so you can stand behind it tomorrow — true past the moment's heat.
steps: Ask if you'll believe this telling next week.
Feel whether it's true or just effective.
Tell the version that ages well.
cards.primaryQuestion: Will I stand behind this story when the moment cools?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the telling I won't regret?
cards.optimizesFor: Durable truth over a moment's impact.
forbiddenMoves: Exaggerating for effect
Bending facts to land harder
Pressuring belief with drama
failureModes: The viral lie you can't walk back
cards.remediation: Trim any claim you couldn't defend later.

tell.working: It holds up on a reread.
tell.performed: You optimize for the reaction, not the record.

contraindications: When precision matters more than narrative
cards.example: You resist the tempting exaggeration and tell the true version that still moves people.

cards.name: Leave a Story That Lasts
cards.essence: Tell it so something true remains after you're gone from the room.
steps: Name the meaning worth remembering — the one you feel in your gut.
Put it where it can outlive the moment (a record, a ritual, a retelling).
Hand it to others to carry.
cards.primaryQuestion: What story do I want to outlast me here?
cards.campaignQuestion: What meaning should remain when this campaign ends?
cards.optimizesFor: A lasting narrative artifact, not a spent performance.
forbiddenMoves: Ephemeral spectacle
Hoarding the story
Guilt-tripping the next generation with it
failureModes: A great night no one remembers
cards.remediation: Write it down and give it away.

tell.performed: You perform a finale and leave nothing.

contraindications: When the moment should stay ephemeral
cards.example: You turn the effort's story into a short written piece others can share.
